name: Cream of Spinach Soup
tagline: Smooth wilted-spinach soup with garlic and pepper — iron + folate + K
highlight: Wilt the spinach for 60 seconds, then blend hot — keeps the bright green colour

hero.alt: Vivid green spinach soup in a bowl with cracked black pepper and a swirl of cream
hero.caption: spinach soup — bright, smooth, peppery

healthFacts: Spinach: 6.4mg iron and 260µg folate per 200g — both at clinically meaningful levels.
Vitamin K (~888µg/cup) supports bone and clotting health.
Lemon at the finish doubles iron absorption from the meal.
Low-calorie (~80 kcal serving) — fits any cardiac-protection plan.

steps.title: Sauté and wilt
steps.detail: Melt butter in a pot. Sauté onion and garlic till soft (3 min). Add spinach in batches, wilting 1 minute total — don't overcook.
steps.tip: Hot, fast wilt = bright green soup. Slow simmering turns it brown.

steps.title: Add stock, blend hot
steps.detail: Pour in stock, simmer 2 minutes. Off heat. Carefully blend in a high-speed blender (or with a stick blender) till silky smooth.
steps.tip: Vent the blender lid — hot soup builds pressure.

steps.title: Finish creamy
steps.detail: Return to pot, stir in milk, salt, and pepper. Warm gently — don't boil. Squeeze lemon at the table for the iron-absorption boost.
steps.tip: A drizzle of olive oil on top adds vitamin E and richness without dairy.
